description: §52.8 SSR first-paint render (D1/D2, S234-235) — a server-authority <each> whose rows are pre-rendered INTO the first-paint HTML (keyed data-scrml-key) so view-source contains the rows before client JS. This case pins the COMPILE half (the canonical SSR source compiles clean — no E-*). RUNTIME-HALF-PENDING: the composed first-paint markup assertion needs the server compose handler (_scrml_ssr_compose_handler) + a window.__scrml_ssr_state seed, which the current client-mount adapter does not invoke — an ADAPTER EXTENSION (track B, escalated). The runtime shape is proven meanwhile by the D2 browser test.
rationale: §52.8: server-authoritative reactive variables SHALL be included in the SSR output; the D1 server-side row renderer (codegen/emit-ssr-render.ts) emits per-row HTML keyed data-scrml-key into the each-mount. The conformance runtime half is adapter-gated: the adapter mounts the CLIENT bundle and has no SSR-compose path; extending it (invoke the compose handler + seed __scrml_ssr_state, borrowing the D2 browser harness) closes this. Codes half only for now.
